notes: All R1..R12 EARS satisfied. Chat POST hits /api/chat/sessions/:id/messages with body {text, context:{}} and asserts message.id+role+text (smoke-api.cjs:473-499). TTS POST hits /api/chat/messages/:id/tts with expected:[200,204,501] (line 504-511). 501+FEATURE_UNAVAILABLE -> R7 message (line 513-516). 204 -> R6 message (line 517-520). Magic-byte check (ID3 OR 0xFF&0xE0) at 543-544. Length floor >=1024 at 554-556. Happy log at 559-561 matches AC9 regex /^\[smoke:api\] tts OK \(bytes=\d+, contentType=audio\/[a-z0-9.+\-]+, msgId=[0-9a-f]{8}\)$/. Cleanup in finally (line 562-577).

Top-level catch (smoke-api.cjs:582-586) concatenates '[smoke:api] FAIL: ' + error.message so R7 surfaces exactly '[smoke:api] FAIL: TTS unavailable (501 FEATURE_UNAVAILABLE)' and R6 surfaces '[smoke:api] FAIL: TTS returned 204 (empty assistant text)'. Matches spec literal wording verbatim.
13 contract tests all green (1 it.todo placeholder for future helper extraction). pnpm test --testPathPattern matched only this file: 13 passed, 1 todo, 0 failed.

Minor: AC9 regex contentType class is [a-z0-9.+\-]+ which would not match if backend ever returns 'audio/mpeg; charset=utf-8'. Today the route sends bare 'audio/mpeg' per createTtsHandler so no actual risk, but worth noting as future drift. Not a blocker since the smoke would also fail in that case via R3 contentType check, but the test contract may need widening.

Diff touches exactly 3 files per spec scope IN: docs/roadmap-night/specs/R5.md (the spec itself), museum-backend/scripts/smoke-api.cjs (the only impl file), tests/unit/scripts/smoke-api-tts-contract.test.ts (T1). Zero src/ modifications. git diff HEAD~1 HEAD -- museum-frontend/ museum-web/ returned empty — no cross-worktree collision.

Zero new env vars. Grep of process.env in the script shows only baseline SMOKE_* keys (SMOKE_API_BASE_URL, SMOKE_TEST_EMAIL/PASSWORD, SMOKE_TIMEOUT_MS, SMOKE_HEALTH_RETRIES/DELAY_MS, SMOKE_ALLOW_REGISTER, SMOKE_TEST_FIRSTNAME/LASTNAME, SMOKE_REQUIRE_REDIS, SMOKE_COMPARE_ENABLED). All pre-existed at HEAD~1. R12/N1/AC4 satisfied.
Zero new package.json scripts, zero new GitHub Actions workflow files, zero .env.example edits — verified.
Sequence ordering correct (AC6): compare OK -> tts OK -> cleanup delete session OK. Verified by the test 'tts OK appears between compare OK and cleanup delete session OK'.

fetchBinary (smoke-api.cjs:113-167) is a clean parallel of fetchJson with the only meaningful divergence being arrayBuffer/Buffer.from instead of JSON.parse. Try/finally for AbortController matches the existing pattern. Returns the same status/json shape on error so callers can introspect error.code uniformly. Reasonable design choice per §3.6 D5 (justified — fetchJson would throw on MP3 binary).
try/finally around the TTS block keeps the cleanup invariant clear without nested catch/rethrow. Reads top-to-bottom.
Magic-byte check is 4 lines (lines 543-549) — minimal, no ffprobe dep, no library. Matches §3.3 D2 doctrine.

Minor over-engineering: fetchBinary returns a `rawText: ''` empty field on success path (line 158) which is unused by the only caller. Could be omitted (or moved to undefined). Cosmetic only. Also, expected:[200,204,501] is a slightly noisy way to opt out of the generic 'unexpected status' branch in fetchBinary — the same flow could be written with expected:200 and a try/catch but the explicit allow-list is arguably cleaner.

Finally-block shadows the original error if DELETE also throws (JS gotcha). Spec explicitly allows finally pattern (R10 alternatives), so not a blocker — but worth a backlog note: wrap the DELETE in its own try/catch and console.error the cleanup failure independently to preserve the TTS root-cause.
N/A — BE-only CLI script, no UI surface. No a11y or design-system concern applies. Scored 100 as default per review brief.

Fail-loud doctrine respected: R7 (501 FEATURE_UNAVAILABLE) raises with the exact contractual wording — no silent skip, no try/catch swallow (smoke-api.cjs:513-516). No SMOKE_TTS_ENABLED toggle, confirmed by env grep. UFR-013 + feedback_no_feature_flags_prelaunch satisfied.
No secrets logged: contentType + bytes + msgId (8 hex prefix only) — no token, no email, no env value. Truncation to 8 chars is benign (UUID prefix is not a credential).

Body preview on unexpected status (line 523-526) slices JSON.stringify to 300 chars — sufficient cap to avoid log explosion, no risk of leaking secrets since the response body is from the smoke account's own session.
AbortController + timer cleanup on every fetchBinary path (try/finally clearTimeout) — no resource leak, no timing channel.
Zero `as any`, zero `@ts-ignore`, zero `eslint-disable` in smoke-api.cjs and the new test file. grep returned empty.
Lint passed end-to-end: eslint src/ --max-warnings=0, then lint:test-discipline, then tsc --noEmit — all clean. Test ran 13 PASS + 1 todo, 0 failures.

niceToHave: Cosmetic: drop `rawText: ''` from fetchBinary success-path return (unused).

Hardening (R10 edge): in the finally block, wrap the DELETE in a local try/catch that console.error's the cleanup failure but does NOT rethrow when the outer try threw — preserves the TTS root-cause stack instead of letting the cleanup error mask it. Spec explicitly allows finally so it's not a blocker, but worth a follow-up.

AC9 widen: if the backend ever begins returning `audio/mpeg; charset=...`, both the inline check (startsWith 'audio/') and the test regex character class `[a-z0-9.+\-]+` would diverge. Today the route sends bare media type so no actual risk — note for future.
Consider extracting the magic-byte validator into a tiny exported helper for the it.todo placeholder once a second caller emerges (don't pre-extract for one site).
